about.name: Lisandra Matos
about.title: About Me
intro: I grew up in a fishing community in Maranhão, Brazil, which established my deep connection to the ocean and its importance for livelihoods. As the first person in my family to access higher education, I graduated with a BSc in Oceanography and completed an MSc in Marine Environmental Protection in the UK. This journey cemented my focus on science-based solutions that balance ecological protection with the economic necessities of the global energy transition.

In my role as a Graduate Fisheries Liaison Officer in Scotland, I specialised in mitigating the impacts of offshore energy development. I led mediation between wind farm developers and key UK fishing organisations, such as the SFF and NFFO. My work was critical: conducting Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) and ensuring local fishing communities' socio-economic concerns were integrated into policy and mitigation strategies, promoting a responsible transition to renewables.

I am currently a Research Assistant II: Renewable Energy at the Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI) in Bogotá, Colombia. My primary focus is applied research and policy analysis to support the inclusive and responsible deployment of renewable energy across Latin America. At SEI, I aim to connect the technical, social, and environmental dimensions of the energy transition, providing practical solutions for a sustainable energy future in the region.
cv_intro: I am a Ph.D. candidate in Computing Science, focusing on developing algorithms for optimisation and machine learning problems. I am adept at translating abstract mathematical concepts into efficient algorithms for complex computational problems such as scheduling, feature engineering, classification and clustering. Beyond theoretical research, I am keen to apply my algorithmic expertise to emerging technologies in Machine Learning and AI, specifically in optimising the underlying data structures and computational graphs that power large-scale distributed systems.

I have more than 5 years of teaching experience in math and algorithms at an undergraduate level. Furthermore, I have experience as a Data Mining Analyst where I successfully implemented the mRMR feature selection algorithm using PySpark to optimise churn prediction models, demonstrating my ability to apply advanced algorithmic research to solve practical big data challenges.
Outside the world of computing science, I enjoy playing football with friends and watching football matches, especially those of my favourite team, Olimpia, Paraguay.

projects.name: Graph Spectral Sparsifier
projects.description: During my master's course, I implemented different graphs spectral sparsifiers in Python and C++. The implementations can be found on my GitHub.

projects.name: mRMR Feature Selection
projects.description: During my year in the industry, I implemented a feature selection algorithm called Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR) in PySpark to improve the performance of a churn prediction model. The implementation can be found on my GitHub.

projects.name: Algorithms for the b-chromatic colouring of graphs
projects.description: During my Ph.D., I implemented several algorithms for the b-chromatic colouring problem in Python. The implementation can be found on my GitHub.

education.institution: Bangor University, School of Ocean Science
education.description: The course offered a comprehensive view of the marine environment, including field trips and studies on the social aspects of the ocean for people and industry. The dissertation research was titled: "Assessing the vulnerability of the UK fishing fleet to climate change". This research specifically focused on addressing the critical issues facing the fisheries sector and to develop specialised knowledge concerning main commercial fishing species, principal UK ports, and the characteristics of fishing vessels.

The curriculum provided a broad, interdisciplinary foundation. Key modules covered essential areas such as: Fundamental Sciences: Chemical, Physical, Biological, and Geological Oceanography. Analytical Techniques: Data Analysis and Processing, Remote Detection, and Meteorology and Climatology. Environmental Policy: Environmental Law and Marine Pollution. The dissertation focused on marine toxicology. The research was titled: "Evaluation of the toxicity of mixtures of biocides and metals to embryos of Crassostrea rhirophorae and bacteria Vibrio fischeri". This project developed skills in laboratory research and the assessment of chemical impacts on marine organisms.

experience.company: School of Computing Science, University of Glasgow
experience.description: I am responsible for leading lab tutorials for the undergraduate courses Algorithmic Foundations and Algorithms and Data Structures. In Algorithmic Foundations, students learn about discrete mathematics, proof methods, and graph theory. In Algorithms and Data Structures, students learn about sorting algorithms, complexity analysis, and data structures like linked lists and trees.

Implemented the Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR) feature selection algorithm in PySpark to optimize a churn prediction model. Conducted research on state-of-the-art feature selection techniques and applied them to large-scale datasets using Apache Spark. Successfully enhanced model performance by identifying key predictive features. The implementation is open-source and available on GitHub.

experience.company: National University of Asunción, Engineering School, Paraguay
experience.description: I have been a teaching assistant on the course of algorithms on the undergraduate course of Mechatronic Engineering. My weekly duties were preparing classes notes on undergraduate topics such as sorting algorithms, divide and conquer algorithms, greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, linear programming, fundamental graph algorithms and the game tree and alpha-beta pruning algorithms.
